subject: Social Sciences
title: Rivers & Resources
tagline: Trace SA rivers and understand their role in settlements!

pins.name: Orange River
facts: The Orange River is South Africa's longest river.
The Orange River forms part of the border with Namibia.

pins.name: Vaal River
facts: The Vaal River is a major tributary of the Orange River.
The Vaal River supplies water to Gauteng industry and homes.

pins.name: Limpopo River
facts: The Limpopo River forms much of the border with Zimbabwe and Botswana.
The Limpopo River basin supports farming across several countries.

pins.name: Tugela River
facts: The Tugela River flows through KwaZulu-Natal.
The Tugela Falls is one of the tallest waterfalls in the world.

pins.name: Breede River
facts: The Breede River flows through the Western Cape winelands.
The Breede River is popular for canoeing.

pins.name: Olifants River
facts: The Olifants River flows through the Kruger National Park.
The Olifants River is important for irrigation in Mpumalanga.
